texto: Music is where I feel loved. Past, present. Music is where I give love. Why do I continue to enter rooms of strangers who are suffering, dying, cursing, diminished, unwashed? Because of love. I don't see hollow faces, blank stares, decaying bodies. I see the faces of God in these human beings. Precious people with stories, contributions, presence. Music pays tribute to their lives, often coaxes out their life stories, gives them worth, but most of all loves them when they are lost, weak, vulnerable.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Robin Russell Gaiser que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Music is where I feel loved. Past, present. Music is where I give love. Why do I continue to ente…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, music, where, com foco em reflexao.
